hypothesis: If metal lattice templates with strong interstitial electron localization (quasi-atoms) are identified and screened by a chemically informed stack-ensemble ML model, then the resulting hydrogen-rich compounds will be confirmed by DFT as thermodynamically stable superhydrides and by electron-phonon calculations as possessing superconducting properties consistent with rapid descriptor predictions, because interstitial electron localization governs both high-pressure stability and electron–phonon coupling.
